title: Generate project skill without a live Unity bridge (offline skill authoring)

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.body: The Unity Editor is CLOSED (no bridge running). mcp-server/dist/index.js is built. The target project exists on disk (the skill generator reads project state from files, not a live bridge). This scenario validates that generate_skill works without a live Unity connection — it composes the template playbook with a project-specific inventory section and writes the file to the client skill directory.

steps.title: Generate the project skill (write:true, no bridge)
Expected outcomes (skill file written)
items: generate_skill returns isError:false (no bridge required — it routes local)
The response reports the skill file path (under the client skill directory, e.g. .claude/skills/unity-open-mcp/SKILL.md)
The written SKILL.md contains the project inventory section: Unity version, installed packages, available tools, key MonoBehaviour/ScriptableObject types
The written SKILL.md contains the mutate→gate→fix workflow playbook (when include_workflow:true)
No 'bridge offline' or 'editor not running' error — generate_skill is local-routed

steps.title: Read back the written skill to confirm content
Expected outcomes (content verified)
items: script_read returns the first 50 lines of the skill file
The content includes a project inventory header (Unity version + package list)
The content is clean of internal references (no specs/ paths, no milestone IDs, no reference-project names)

steps.title: Remove the generated skill (so the scenario is re-runnable)
Paste the agent output (generate_skill + script_read envelopes)

reset.note: Optional scenario. generate_skill is local-routed (no bridge dependency) — this validates the offline authoring path and the template/inventory merge. The cleanup deletes the written skill so the scenario is re-runnable; in real use the skill persists.
